summary: I revised `requirements.md` for iteration 2, addressing all five gaps flagged by the requirements-completeness critic (F1–F5) using the fresh code facts from the iteration-2 discovery notes: added requirements that Explore/Clarifying Questions/Repeat-from-Beginning results are actually shown to the person before they choose their next action (R9, R11, R18), specified that a "Repeat from the Beginning" restart runs several steps in sequence with a "restart in progress" state before the menu reappears (R15–R18), added a dedicated failure-path section for menu-triggered re-runs (R24–R25), gated "Done" on Define having reached its settled state with an explicit not-ready message otherwise (R19–R20), and split the two compound requirements (old R13, R23) into independently testable ones (R16/R17, R31/R32/R33). The result is 33 atomic, non-technical "When X, Y should happen" requirements plus an updated assumptions list reflecting the new resolutions.
